content.problemStatement:
Create a variable named **speed1** and assign it the value 36, representing speed in kilometers per hour. Then, convert and display this speed in meters per second.

[**Note:** 1 km/h = 5/18 m/s]

**Output:**

`10
`
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains an integer speed1 (1 ≤ speed1 ≤ 1000) representing speed in km/h.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the speed converted to meters per second, rounded down to the nearest integer.

solution.approach: The conversion factor from km/h to m/s is 5/18. For each given speed1 we need floor(speed1 * 5 / 18). Because all numbers are integers, integer division (speed1*5)/18 automatically gives the floor value. The algorithm is a single pass over the T test cases, performing a constant‑time arithmetic operation for each case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case.

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T; if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1) return 0; while(T--){int speed1; if(scanf("%d",&speed1)!=1) break; int result = (speed1*5)/18; printf("%d\n",result);} return 0;}
